title: Authors and Citation
text: Park S (2026). HARMONIC: HARMONIC: Ontology-Guided Tree Structuring Multi-Case Integration GO Enrichment.. R package version 0.99.0, https://github.com/ERASMUSlab/HARMONIC, https://erasmuslab.github.io/HARMONIC.
code: @Manual{,   title = {HARMONIC: HARMONIC: Ontology-Guided Tree Structuring and Multi-Case Integration for GO Enrichment.},   author = {Seung Gwa Park},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.99.0, https://github.com/ERASMUSlab/HARMONIC},   url = {https://erasmuslab.github.io/HARMONIC}, }

title: HARMONIC
text: HARMONIC R package Gene Ontology (GO) Biological Process (BP) enrichment analysis helps reduce redundancy improve interpretability top-ranked results. Instead reporting long lists highly overlapping terms, HARMONIC restructures enriched terms ontology-guided term trees using GO DAG, enabling compact summaries biological signals. release provides following key features: Ontology-based structuring reduce redundant top terms: HARMONIC reorganizes enriched BP terms tree-structured groups based GO hierarchy term relationships, helping prevent near-duplicate terms dominating top ranks. Active-tree filtering suppress structural false positives: GO hierarchical, significant-looking terms can appear due dependency rather true signal. HARMONIC defines filters active trees using coverage/consistency criteria (e.g., hit-term count hit ratio) downweight structurally driven results. Single-case multiple-case workflows: HARMONIC supports single-condition summaries multiple-case designs integrating enrichment outputs across conditions. enables discovery common vs condition-specific biological processes facilitates trend/pattern interpretation across dose, time-course, cohort comparisons. Visualization utilities tree-level interpretation: HARMONIC provides plotting functions summarize active trees term–gene relationships, including single- multiple-case views interpretation reporting. Install: Install Data Preprocessing & Analysis: Single case & Multiple case Visualization: Single case & Multiple case

what: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
title: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
text: Orchestrates preprocessing (optional), term listing, comparison, separation. Supports single- multi-condition workflows.

title: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
code: HARMONIC_ANALYSIS(   filepath,   DEG_list_name,   ref_genome,   core = 1,   PASSED_RATIO,   PASSED_NUM,   similarity,   FC = 2,   condition = 1,   dynamic_analyisis = "F",   preprocessing = "F" )

title: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
text: filepath Working directory path. DEG_list_name DEG list filename filepath. ref_genome Reference genome code ("mm" "hs"). core Number cores. PASSED_RATIO Passed dependency ratio threshold. PASSED_NUM Passed dependency count threshold. similarity Similarity cutoff (%). FC Fold-change ratio cutoff. condition condition index dynamic analysis. dynamic_analyisis "T" "F" enable multi-range filtering. preprocessing "T" "F" run preprocessing inside function.

title: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
text: Writes output files disk. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Run the HARMONIC analysis pipeline — HARMONIC_ANALYSIS
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_ANALYSIS)) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ HARMONIC_ANALYSIS(   filepath=".",   DEG_list_name="input_DEG_list.txt",   ref_genome="mm",   core=1,   PASSED_RATIO=15,   PASSED_NUM=4,   similarity=70 ) } # }

what: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
text: HARMONIC tree (term dependency string), maps GO descriptions calculates per-condition score using rich factor DEG fold-change information, producing comparison matrix.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
code: HARMONIC_COMPARE(   input_TERMLISTING,   filepath,   fileTABLE_path,   DEG_list_name,   GOTABLE_path,   outputPATH )

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
text: input_TERMLISTING Path tree/subtree table produced term listing. filepath Base directory containing DEG_list_name. fileTABLE_path Path table listing HARMONIC preprocessing files (one per row). DEG_list_name DEG list filename filepath. GOTABLE_path Path GO list table written preprocessing. outputPATH Output path write comparison table.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
text: Writes outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores across multiple cases — HARMONIC_COMPARE
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_COMPARE))

what: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
text: Single-condition version HARMONIC_COMPARE. Produces one-column comparison table single case.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
code: HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE(   input_TERMLISTING,   filepath,   fileTABLE_path,   DEG_list_name,   GOTABLE_path,   PASSED_NUM,   outputPATH )

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
text: input_TERMLISTING Path tree/subtree table produced term listing. filepath Base directory containing DEG_list_name. fileTABLE_path Path table listing single HARMONIC preprocessing file. DEG_list_name DEG list filename filepath. GOTABLE_path Path GO list table written preprocessing. PASSED_NUM Threshold passed dependency count (column 3). outputPATH Output path write comparison table.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
text: Writes outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Compute HARMONIC tree scores for a single case — HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_COMPARE_SINGLE))

what: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
title: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
text: Create heatmap HARMONIC output tables (HARMONIC_SEPERATE HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range).

title: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
code: HARMONIC_HeatMap(   filepath,   DEG_list_name,   trends,   condition = "ALL",   width,   height,   max,   dynamic_analyisis = "F" )

title: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
text: filepath Working directory path. DEG_list_name DEG list filename filepath. trends One "", "", "SIG" (controls color palette). condition "", "SIG", specific condition index/value used last column HARMONIC_SEPERATE output. width Plot width (notebook display options). height Plot height (notebook display options). max Maximum value heatmap color scale (used breaks). dynamic_analyisis "F" single/multi heatmap using HARMONIC_SEPERATE, "T" dynamic analysis heatmap using HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range.

title: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
text: pheatmap object.

title: Draw a heatmap of HARMONIC results — HARMONIC_HeatMap
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_HeatMap)) # \donttest{ # HARMONIC_HeatMap( #   filepath = ".", #   DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt", #   trends = "UP", #   condition = "ALL", #   width = 10, #   height = 6, #   max = 5, #   dynamic_analyisis = "F" # ) # }

what: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
title: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
text: Reads DEG lists, runs clusterProfiler::enrichGO (BP), writes per-case GO tables, calls preprocessing shell script writes resulting preprocessing list.

title: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
code: HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING(   DEG_list_path,   GO_list_path,   HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list_path,   ref_genome,   core,   filepath )

title: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
text: DEG_list_path Path table listing DEG files (one file per row). GO_list_path Output path write list generated GO result files. HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list_path Output path write list generated HARMONIC preprocessing result files. ref_genome Reference genome code. Use "mm" mouse "hs" human. core Number cores pass preprocessing shell script. filepath Base directory CODE/init_HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING.sh exists.

title: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
text: Writes output files disk. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Run GO enrichment preprocessing for HARMONIC — HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING)) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING(   DEG_list_path = "input_DEG_list.txt",   GO_list_path = "input_DEG_GO_list.txt",   HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list_path = "HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list.txt",   ref_genome = "mm",   core = 1,   filepath = "." ) } # }

what: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
title: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
text: function separates HARMONIC terms condition using comparison matrix, applies additional filtering based number matched GO terms per tree (>= PASSED_NUM) condition non-separated terms.

title: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
code: HARMONIC_SEPERATE(   input_COMPARE,   fileTABLE_path,   GOTABLE_path,   FC,   PASSED_NUM,   outputPATH )

title: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
text: input_COMPARE Path HARMONIC_COMPARE output file. fileTABLE_path Path HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list file (condition table). GOTABLE_path Path GO result list table (GO_list_path). FC Fold-change ratio cutoff used separating condition-specific terms. PASSED_NUM Minimum number matched GO terms required keep tree. outputPATH Output path write separated table.

title: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
text: Writes tab-delimited file outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Separate HARMONIC terms by condition specificity (with PASS/FAIL filtering) — HARMONIC_SEPERATE
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_SEPERATE)) # \donttest{ # HARMONIC_SEPERATE( #   input_COMPARE = "HARMONIC_COMPARE.txt", #   fileTABLE_path = "HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list.txt", #   GOTABLE_path = "input_DEG_GO_list.txt", #   FC = 2, #   PASSED_NUM = 4, #   outputPATH = "HARMONIC_SEPERATE.txt" # ) # }

what: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
title: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
text: Given HARMONIC_COMPARE table across multiple conditions, function selects trees active specified condition indices satisfy fold-change separation criterion versus remaining conditions.

title: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
code: HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA(input_COMPARE, fileTABLE_path, FC, outputPATH, condition)

title: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
text: input_COMPARE Path HARMONIC_COMPARE result file (tab-delimited). fileTABLE_path Path HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list file (tab-delimited). FC Fold-change threshold DA criterion. outputPATH Output file path write DA-filtered result (tab-delimited). condition Integer vector 1-based condition indices define POS set.

title: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
text: Writes table outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Differential-activity (DA) tree filtering for selected conditions — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA)) # \donttest{ # HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA( #   input_COMPARE = "HARMONIC_COMPARE.txt", #   fileTABLE_path = "HARMONIC_PREPROCESSING_list.txt", #   filepath = ".", #   DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt", #   FC = 2, #   outputPATH = "HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forDA.txt", #   condition = c(1, 2) # ) # }

what: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
title: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
text: Filters HARMONIC comparison results terms present selected conditions (condition_A:condition_B) exceeding FC ratio remaining conditions.

title: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
code: HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range(   input_COMPARE,   fileTABLE_path,   FC,   outputPATH,   condition_A,   condition_B )

title: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
text: input_COMPARE Path HARMONIC comparison table. fileTABLE_path Path table listing conditions (used determine number columns). FC Fold-change ratio cutoff used filtering. outputPATH Output path write filtered table. condition_A Start condition index (1-based, matching code convention). condition_B End condition index (1-based, matching code convention).

title: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
text: Writes outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Select significant HARMONIC terms within a condition range — HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_SEPERATE_forMULTI_range))

what: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
title: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
text: Aggregates total_Dependency strings across preprocessing outputs, computes pairwise overlap ratio, creates tree/subtree mapping based similarity threshold.

title: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
code: HARMONIC_TERMLISTING(   PASSED_RATIO,   PASSED_NUM,   similarity,   fileTABLE_path,   outputPATH )

title: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
text: PASSED_RATIO Threshold Ratio_of_passed_Dependency (column 4). PASSED_NUM Threshold passed dependency count (column 3). similarity Similarity cutoff (%) used define similar trees. fileTABLE_path Path table listing HARMONIC preprocessing files (one per row). outputPATH Output path write final tree/subtree table.

title: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
text: Writes outputPATH. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Build the HARMONIC term tree list across multiple cases — HARMONIC_TERMLISTING
code: stopifnot(is.function(HARMONIC_TERMLISTING))

what: Plot HARMONIC terms for a single tree (bar or chord) — HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE
title: Plot HARMONIC terms for a single tree (bar or chord) — HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE
text: Generate bar plot (term scores) chord diagram (term–gene links) selected tree HARMONIC single-case outputs.

title: Plot HARMONIC terms for a single tree (bar or chord) — HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE
code: HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE(   filepath,   DEG_list_name,   plot,   trends,   clusternum,   width,   height,   LABEL = "text for result",   lfc_leng = 2 )

title: Plot HARMONIC terms for a single tree (bar or chord) — HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE
text: filepath Working directory path. DEG_list_name DEG list filename filepath. plot Plot type: "bar" "cir". trends One "", "", "SIG". clusternum Tree (cluster) index plot. width Plot width (notebook display options). height Plot height (notebook display options). LABEL Title label plot. lfc_leng Max abs LFC used chord color scaling.

title: Plot HARMONIC terms for a single tree (bar or chord) — HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE
text: plot="bar", returns ggplot object (prints ). plot="cir", returns NULL invisibly (circos draws device).

what: HARMONIC multi-case term bar plot (term-level) — HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI
title: HARMONIC multi-case term bar plot (term-level) — HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI
text: Draw bar plot GO terms within selected HARMONIC tree multi-case results. function supports three modes via status: "common", "specific", "DA". Bars represent term score (tscore-like), dashed segments/points encode gene distribution ratio median fold-change genes term (implemented original logic).

title: HARMONIC multi-case term bar plot (term-level) — HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI
code: HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI(   filepath,   DEG_list_name,   status,   trends,   LABEL,   condition,   clusternum,   width,   height )

title: HARMONIC multi-case term bar plot (term-level) — HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI
text: filepath Directory path containing input files. DEG_list_name DEG list table filename filepath. status One "common", "specific", "DA". trends One "", "", "SIG". LABEL Character vector case labels (used titles). condition Condition index (used pick case/contrast). clusternum Tree(cluster) index display. width Plot width (used repr.plot.width option notebooks). height Plot height (used repr.plot.height option notebooks).

title: HARMONIC multi-case term bar plot (term-level) — HARMONIC_TERM_barPLOT_forMULTI
text: ggplot object.

title: HARMONIC multi-case circular plot (tree-level) — HARMONIC_TREE_cirPLOT_forMULTI
code: HARMONIC_TREE_cirPLOT_forMULTI(   filepath,   DEG_list_name,   status,   trends,   LABEL,   project = "plot\nname",   condition = 1,   tree_size,   label_size,   project_size,   width,   height )

title: HARMONIC multi-case circular plot (tree-level) — HARMONIC_TREE_cirPLOT_forMULTI
text: filepath Directory path containing input files. DEG_list_name DEG list table filename filepath. status One "common", "specific", "DA". trends One "", "", "SIG". LABEL Character vector case labels. project Project label shown plot. condition Condition index (used "specific"). tree_size Text size tree labels. label_size Text size individual labels. project_size Text size project label. width Plot width (used repr.plot.width option notebooks). height Plot height (used repr.plot.height option notebooks).

title: HARMONIC multi-case circular plot (tree-level) — HARMONIC_TREE_cirPLOT_forMULTI
text: ggplot object.
